prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel vehicle called a klon has a property: it can be activated by switching the panel at a specific position on it. Your job is to figure out which employee in the perception scenario is the klon-analog, and therefore which specialty on it activates it.

Memory scenario: There are three vehicles in a lot: a klon, a vro, and a yib. The klon is-parked-to-the-left-of the vro. The vro is-stacked-below the yib. The klon has a cyan colored panel on top, a red colored panel on side, and a green colored panel on bottom. The vro has a orange colored panel on top, a black colored panel on side, and a yellow colored panel on bottom. The yib has a brown colored panel on top, a blue colored panel on side, and a purple colored panel on bottom. Switching the panel at the top of the klon activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three employees in an organization: a oxim, a glim, and a jax. The glim reports-to the jax. The jax sits-beside the oxim. The oxim has a cyan color-coded specialty on top, a red color-coded specialty on side, and a green color-coded specialty on bottom. The glim has a brown color-coded specialty on top, a blue color-coded specialty on side, and a purple color-coded specialty on bottom. The jax has a yellow color-coded specialty on top, a black color-coded specialty on side, and a orange color-coded specialty on bottom.

Which employee in the perception scenario is the klon-analog, and which specialty activates it? Answer as a fenced JSON block with keys `analog` (the perception employee's name) and `button_color` (the color of the activating specialty on that employee).
